title: Gooey Herbal Korean Knafah Brochette
description: A sweet and savory Korean-inspired dessert featuring crispy knafeh, gooey herbal filling, and a lazy Sunday vibe. This nut-free-friendly treat is perfect for a relaxing afternoon.
instructions: Cut the knafeh dough into 1-inch (2.5cm) pieces and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the sesame oil over the knafeh pieces and toss to coat.
Broil the knafeh for 2-3 minutes or until golden brown, flipping halfway through.
In a small saucepan, combine the herbal tea, honey, and Korean chili flakes. Heat over low heat, stirring until the honey dissolves.
To assemble the brochette, thread a piece of knafeh onto a skewer, followed by a spoonful of the herbal honey mixture, and a sprinkle of shredded coconut.
Repeat the layers two more times, ending with a piece of knafeh.
Serve the brochette with a scoop of green tea ice cream and garnish with chopped fresh mint leaves.
Drizzle any remaining herbal honey mixture over the top.
Enjoy immediately, as the knafeh will become soggy if left to sit.
